data.title: Northern Trust: Leveraging ‘One Northern Trust,’ Capital Strength, and Visa Monetization to Drive EPS Growth and Justify a Premium Valuation
data.description:
BMO Capital analyst Brennan Hawken upgraded the rating on Northern to a Buy today, setting a price target of $168.00.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Brennan Hawken has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Northern Trust’s improving earnings profile and strategic reorientation. Management’s “One Northern Trust” initiative is shifting the business mix toward higher-margin wealth and asset management, while a renewed focus on productivity, AI-driven automation, and centralized cost oversight is driving sustainable operating leverage and better expense control.

He also highlights the bank’s robust capital position, with CET1 materially above regulatory and internal targets, which provides scope for enhanced shareholder returns and selective acquisitions that can bolster growth. Additionally, pending monetization of the Visa stake is expected to be directed mainly toward buybacks and targeted wealth management deals rather than heavy new spending, supporting EPS accretion; combined with resilient balance-sheet trends and a differentiated franchise, these elements justify a premium valuation and an Outperform rating.

In another report released on March 26, RBC Capital also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $159.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Emerging Market Price Resets Threaten Unilever’s 2026 Earnings and Margins
data.description:
Jefferies analyst David Hayes maintained a Sell rating on Unilever today and set a price target of p4,300.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Hayes has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors tied to Unilever’s pricing actions in emerging markets. He highlights that the company has begun cutting or resetting prices in places like Thailand to ease pressure on cash‑strained consumers facing higher oil and living costs, which signals a shift away from pure price‑led growth.

He argues that similar moves are likely to spread across other Asian markets, which would support volumes but squeeze profitability in the near term. In his view, this dynamic creates meaningful downside risk to 2026 earnings, especially as the market consensus still largely expects Unilever to continue expanding both prices and margins, leaving estimates vulnerable to negative revisions.

Hayes covers the Consumer Defensive sector, focusing on stocks such as Unilever, Nestlé SA, and Puig Brands, S.A.. According to TipRanks, Hayes has an average return of 2.4% and a 54.75% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 1, UBS also maintained a Sell rating on the stock with a p4,440.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
